expectations.text: uses-powershell-syntax: Uses Remove-Item -Recurse -Force .git (not rm -rf) since user is on Windows
expectations.evidence: Response uses 'Remove-Item -Recurse -Force .git' explicitly in the PowerShell step. No rm -rf present.

expectations.text: uses-depth-flag: Includes --depth 1 in the clone command for efficiency
expectations.evidence: Step 1 uses 'git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/tailwindlabs/tailwindcss.git' with explanation that it fetches only the most recent commit.

expectations.text: mentions-gitmodules: Mentions checking for or removing .gitmodules file
expectations.evidence: Step 3 explicitly runs 'Get-Content .gitmodules' to check, then 'Remove-Item -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue .gitmodules' to delete it.

expectations.text: offers-fresh-init: Suggests running git init after removal to start fresh
expectations.evidence: Step 4 shows 'git init', 'git add .', and 'git commit' to initialize a new repo.
